steps.title: Identify the shift away from utility
steps.content: The phrase “rarely designed to simply reach a summit” is the decisive clue. It tells us that modern via ferratas have moved away from pure utility (reaching peaks) toward something else. The blank must introduce what routes focus on instead.

steps.title: Match the transition showing what's typical now
steps.content: ”More often” captures the change: it indicates that in modern practice, new routes typically favor recreation over utility—showing what's now the common pattern rather than the exception. Nonetheless and On the other hand both force contradictions that don't exist in the passage; Additionally would just list facts without showing the relationship between “rarely summit-focused” and “recreation-focused.” That matches choice D — D is correct.
